All committed content is on the record. 757 758 - **On background:** Vault-safe but identity-protected. Use carefully — this is a public repo. 759 760 - **Off the record:** Ephemeral. Do not log, do not store, do not commit. If Logan says something is off the record, it does not go in files, code, comments, or commit messages. 761 762 763 764 When uncertain about sourcing category, **ask Logan**. 765 766 767 768 --- 769 770 771 772 ## Obsidian Sync / Git Boundary 773 774 Two systems share the vault. They have distinct, non-overlapping responsibilities. 775 776 | Layer | Obsidian Sync | Git / GitHub | 777 | --- | --- | --- | 778 | Vault content (`.md` files) | Syncs across devices | Tracked | 779 | Plugin code (`main.js`, `manifest.json`) | Desktop only (see below) | Tracked — public record | 780 | Plugin settings (`data.json`) | Desktop only, E2E encrypted | **Gitignored** — never reaches GitHub | 781 | `community-plugins.json` | Per-device (not synced) | Tracked — canonical desktop plugin list | 782 | Workspace state (`workspace.json`, `graph.json`) | Per-device | Gitignored — machine-local noise | 783 784 **Rule:** Obsidian Sync is the private courier for credentials and machine state. Git is the public record for vault content and plugin presence. The `.gitignore` wildcard `.obsidian/plugins/*/data.json` is the firewall — no `data.json` reaches GitHub without a deliberate `git add --force`. 785 786 ### Device Roles 787 788 | Device | Role | Community Plugins | 789 | --- | --- | --- | 790 | **Desktop** (Windows) | Engine room — full plugin stack, git, MCP servers, Linter, Breadcrumbs, agent infrastructure | 26 (curated tier) | 791 | **Mobile** (Pixel) | Capture device — quick-jot notepad, read access | 0 (bare Obsidian) | 792 793 The phone writes `.md` files. The desktop processes them. 794 795 ### Obsidian Sync Settings — Desktop (Workspace) 796 797 | Toggle | Setting | 798 | --- | --- | 799 | Core settings | OFF — prevents circular dependency (Sync is a core plugin; syncing core plugin settings makes Sync's own selective-sync config vault-wide instead of per-device) | 800 | Appearance | ON | 801 | Hotkeys | ON | 802 | Active core plugins | ON | 803 | Active community plugins | ON | 804 | Installed community plugins | ON | 805 806 ### Obsidian Sync Settings — Mobile (Pixel — Capture) 807 808 | Toggle | Setting | 809 | --- | --- | 810 | Core settings | OFF — same circular dependency fix; each device controls its own media sync toggles independently | 811 | Appearance | ON | 812 | Hotkeys | ON | 813 | Active core plugins | OFF — phone does not need slides, audio-recorder, webviewer, etc. | 814 | Active community plugins | OFF — decouples plugin lists; desktop keeps 26, phone keeps 0 | 815 | Installed community plugins | OFF — phone does not need 54 plugin directories | 816 817 ### Why Core Plugin Settings Are OFF 818 819 Sync is itself a core plugin. With "Core plugin settings: ON," Sync's selective-sync configuration (audio/video/PDF toggles) propagates between devices — making those toggles vault-wide, not per-device. This creates a circular dependency: the phone needs media sync ON (capture device), but the laptop needs it OFF (workspace). Turning core plugin settings OFF on both devices breaks this circle and lets each device control its own Sync behavior independently. 820 821 ### Why Per-Device Plugin Lists 822 823 Obsidian Sync defaults to pushing the same plugin list to all devices. On a 20K-file vault, the desktop's 26-plugin stack (Dataview indexing, Omnisearch search index, Linter watching saves, MCP tools looking for a REST API server) overwhelms mobile. Disabling community plugin sync on the phone lets each device run its own appropriate stack while content (`.md` files), appearance, and hotkeys still flow both ways. 824 825 ### Conflict Duplicates 826 827 When both devices edit the same config file between syncs, Obsidian creates a `(2)` copy (e.g., `app (2).json`). These are already gitignored via `.obsidian/**(2)*.json`. Delete them when found — Obsidian only reads the original filename. 828 829 830 ## Git Practices 831 832 833 834 - Branch naming: 835 836 - `claude/description-sessionId` for Claude Code branches 837 838 - `copilot/description` for GitHub Copilot branches 839 840 - `gemini/description` for Gemini agent branches 841 842 - Branch lifecycle defaults: 843 844 - Branches are temporary working instances by default, not standing provinces. 845 846 - Resolve each branch explicitly as `merged`, `superseded`, `archived`, 847 `abandoned`, `dormant`, or `reactivated` under the shared lifecycle 848 vocabulary in `CONSTITUTION.md`. 849 850 - A long-lived branch requires a named purpose, a steward, and a review 851 cadence. Defines which capabilities are available in each environment, substitution conventions for gaps, and the MESHWEB Registry of env-scoped artifacts. 955 956 See `MESHWEB.md` for the full standard. 957 958 --- 959 960 ## Portable Path Standard (NETWEB) 961 962 963 964 The vault must work identically on **any platform** — Windows (NTFS), macOS (APFS/HFS+), Linux (ext4), iOS/Android (Obsidian mobile), and CI runners (GitHub Actions). Both NTFS and APFS are **case-insensitive**; only Linux is case-sensitive. This standard targets the **lowest common denominator** of all target filesystems. 965 966 967 968 ### Forbidden filenames (any extension, any case) 969 970 `AUX`, `CON`, `NUL`, `PRN`, `COM0`–`COM9`, `LPT0`–`LPT9` 971 972 These are Windows reserved device names inherited from MS-DOS. They cannot exist as files on NTFS regardless of extension. 973 974 975 976 ### Aliasing convention 977 978 When a stub or note would collide with a reserved name or a case-insensitive duplicate: 979 980 1. Prefix the filename with `_` (e.g., `AUX.md` becomes `_AUX.md`) 981 2. Add `aliases: [ORIGINAL]` to the YAML frontmatter so Obsidian wikilinks (`AUX`) still resolve 982 983 This preserves the connectome while respecting filesystem constraints. 984 985 986 987 ### Case uniqueness 988 989 Filenames within any single directory **must be case-unique**. `Act.md` and `ACT.md` cannot coexist — NTFS and APFS silently overwrite one on checkout. When creating stubs or notes, check for existing files that differ only in case. 990 991 992 993 ### Forbidden path patterns 994 995 - Trailing period (`.`) or space (` `) in any directory or file name 996 - Characters illegal on Windows: `< > : " | ? *` 997 - Colons (`:`) in filenames (illegal on macOS — internal path separator) 998 - Paths exceeding **218 characters** from repo root (NTFS MAX_PATH 260 minus typical local prefix) 999 1000 1001 1002 ### Enforcement 1003 1004 | Layer | Mechanism | Scope | 1005 | --- | --- | --- | 1006 | `.gitignore` | Case-insensitive patterns for reserved names | Advisory — prevents accidental `git add` | 1007 | `check-portable-paths.yml` | CI workflow on every PR and push to `main` | **Hard gate** — blocks merge on violation | 1008 | Agent discipline | All agents must check before creating files | Preventive | 1009 1010 1011 1012 ### Reference 1013 1014 - [Microsoft: Naming Files, Paths, and Namespaces](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/fileio/naming-a-file) 1015 - [Apple File System Guide](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/foundation/file_system) 1016
